summary: The IDF struck Hamas underground infrastructure in Deir al-Balah, central Gaza, after identifying ceasefire violations and attempts to rehabilitate tunnels. In Lebanon, Defense Minister Katz stated the IDF will not withdraw until Hezbollah is disarmed, citing near-total destruction of Shiite villages along the border. Two IRGC members were killed in Paveh, Kermanshah province, in an armed attack claimed by a newly established group.
key_points: IDF struck Hamas underground infrastructure in Deir al-Balah, central Gaza, after identifying ceasefire violations and tunnel rehabilitation efforts. (Source: @idf_telegram/24476)

Defense Minister Katz stated the IDF will not withdraw from Lebanon until Hezbollah is disarmed, citing near-100% destruction of Shiite villages in western and central sectors. (Source: @manniefabian/58707)
Two IRGC members, Barhan Kerisani and Khaled Khalidi, were killed in Paveh, Kermanshah province, in an armed attack at their homes; two others were severely wounded. (Source: @VahidOnline/76749)

Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson stated no negotiations will be held with the US at any level in the coming days, dismissing reports of American envoys traveling to Qatar. (Source: @yediotnews25/132075)
Lebanese MP Hussein Haj Hassan rejected the framework agreement with Israel, calling it a shame and surrender, and stated no one can disarm the resistance. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373649)
IDF Arabic spokesperson released a video declaring Hamas is on its way to disappearing, featuring senior Hamas names marked for elimination. (Source: @inon_yttach/9210)
IDF and Shin Bet announced the killing of Ismail Masri, chief of Hamas military security in the Rafah Brigade, who was targeted last Tuesday in the Gaza Strip. (Source: @manniefabian/58706)

Iran's Supreme National Security Council approved a US-Iran understanding with 11 members voting positively, including the president, parliament head, judiciary chief, IRGC commander Amir Hatami, and two Supreme Leader representatives. (Source: @SharghDaily/168903)

sections.executive_summary: The IDF struck Hamas underground infrastructure in Deir al-Balah, central Gaza, after identifying ceasefire violations and tunnel rehabilitation efforts. In Lebanon, Defense Minister Katz stated the IDF will not withdraw until Hezbollah is disarmed, citing near-total destruction of Shiite villages along the border. Two IRGC members were killed in Paveh, Kermanshah province, in an armed attack claimed by a newly established group called Khurri Hiva.

points: Two IRGC members, Barhan Kerisani and Khaled Khalidi, were killed in Paveh, Kermanshah province, after armed individuals opened fire at their homes; two other IRGC members were severely wounded in the same attack. (Source: @VahidOnline/76749)
The newly established group Khurri Hiva (Sun of Hope) claimed responsibility for the attack on IRGC members in Paveh, according to the human rights organization Hengaw. (Source: @VahidOnline/76749)

Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson stated no negotiations will be held with the US at any level in the coming days, dismissing reports of American envoys traveling to Qatar as unrelated to any Iranian delegation. (Source: @yediotnews25/132075)

Iran's Supreme National Security Council approved a US-Iran understanding with 11 members voting positively, including President, Parliament Head, Judiciary Chief, IRGC Commander Amir Hatami, Foreign Minister, and two Supreme Leader representatives (Saeed Jalili and Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr). (Source: @SharghDaily/168903)

The Supreme Leader stated the Supreme National Security Council meeting should be attended by senior military commanders, and if three-quarters of members voted positively, the understanding would be accepted. (Source: @SharghDaily/168903)
Mohammad Akbarzadeh, political deputy of the IRGC Navy, was killed when his vehicle overturned on a road. (Source: @kianmeli1/88232)
Registration for authorized foreign pilgrims for Arba'een pilgrimage began on 9 Tir (June 30) at foreign nationals' residency and employment offices across Iran. (Source: @SharghDaily/168898)

IDF struck Hamas underground infrastructure in Deir al-Balah, central Gaza, after identifying ceasefire violations and tunnel rehabilitation efforts; three targets were attacked to destroy the infrastructure. (Source: @idf_telegram/24476)

IDF and Shin Bet announced the killing of Ismail Masri, chief of Hamas military security in the Rafah Brigade, who was targeted last Tuesday in the Gaza Strip; he coordinated security and counterintelligence and attempted to rehabilitate military security. (Source: @manniefabian/58706)

Defense Minister Katz stated the IDF will not withdraw from Lebanon until Hezbollah is disarmed, citing near-100% destruction of Shiite villages in western and central sectors and 73% destruction in the eastern sector. (Source: @manniefabian/58707)
Katz stated some 600,000 Shiites from southern Lebanon villages fled and another 700,000 were displaced from Hezbollah's Dahiyeh stronghold in Beirut. (Source: @manniefabian/58707)

Katz confirmed the IDF had planned a massive aerial campaign that would have dismantled Hezbollah, but American pressure linking Iran and Lebanon arenas stopped the operation. (Source: @manniefabian/58707)
A reservist commando fighter was severely injured by an explosion during operational activity in the Kfar Arnon area; the source of the explosion is under investigation. (Source: @inon_yttach/9215)
IDF Arabic spokesperson released a video declaring Hamas is on its way to disappearing, featuring senior Hamas names marked for elimination with a red stamp. (Source: @inon_yttach/9210)

IDF struck Hamas underground infrastructure in Deir al-Balah, central Gaza, after identifying ceasefire violations and tunnel rehabilitation efforts; three targets were attacked to destroy the infrastructure. (Source: @idf_telegram/24476)

IDF and Shin Bet announced the killing of Ismail Masri, chief of Hamas military security in the Rafah Brigade, who was targeted last Tuesday in the Gaza Strip; he coordinated security and counterintelligence and attempted to rehabilitate military security. (Source: @manniefabian/58706)

Hamas systematically places its infrastructure in the heart of the civilian population in Gaza, cynically exploiting residents and using them as human shields to advance terror plans. (Source: @idf_telegram/24476)
A clash occurred in the village of Kfar Yattah overnight. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93311)
A 9-year-old girl was run over and killed in Petah Tikva; the bus driver, a resident of Kafr Qasim, was arrested. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93307)

Defense Minister Katz stated the IDF will not withdraw from Lebanon until Hezbollah is disarmed, citing near-100% destruction of Shiite villages in western and central sectors and 73% destruction in the eastern sector. (Source: @manniefabian/58707)
Katz stated some 600,000 Shiites from southern Lebanon villages fled and another 700,000 were displaced from Hezbollah's Dahiyeh stronghold in Beirut. (Source: @manniefabian/58707)

Lebanese MP Hussein Haj Hassan rejected the framework agreement with Israel, calling it a shame and surrender, and stated no one can disarm the resistance; he criticized the president for negotiating with the enemy and agreeing to bring foreign forces. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373649)

Katz confirmed the IDF tried to get the Lebanese army to enter the Ali Taher Ridge area, under which Hezbollah has a major tunnel system, but the Lebanese army refused; the IDF remains stationed there with some 30 Hezbollah operatives believed holed up underground. (Source: @manniefabian/58707)

Daily statistics for Lebanon recorded 170 total alerts, 153 drone sightings, 2 airstrikes in Deir Siryan, and 5 artillery strikes across multiple villages including Yahmar al-Shaqif, Deir Siryan, Bayt Yahun, Hadatha, and Kfar Tebnit. (Source: @redlinkleb/29102)

Iran insisted that any agreement with the US must include Lebanon with two essential issues: ceasefire and Israeli withdrawal from Lebanese territories; the Lebanese authority rejected this Iranian support in favor of the American-Israeli agreement. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373649)
Lebanese Parliament Speaker Berri appeared before Bassil regarding the Washington agreement as if nothing had happened. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373644)
Raids in the Zayouna area east of Baghdad targeted the home of the Deputy Oil Minister for the second time. (Source: @naya_foriraq/80355)

The al-Jolani government is demolishing all houses in the Shiite village of Al-Mazraa in Homs countryside using tanks and bulldozers, and directing residents to go to Iran or the Dahiya. (Source: @naya_foriraq/80365)
More than 15 million dollars and quantities of gold were found in the home of an Iraqi parliament member during her arrest. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93308)

Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson stated no negotiations will be held with the US at any level in the coming days, dismissing reports of American envoys traveling to Qatar as unrelated to any Iranian delegation. (Source: @yediotnews25/132075)

Defense Minister Katz stated the IDF will not withdraw from Lebanon until Hezbollah is disarmed, citing near-100% destruction of Shiite villages in western and central sectors and 73% destruction in the eastern sector. (Source: @manniefabian/58707)

sections.threat_assessment: The operational environment remains highly volatile across multiple theaters. In Gaza, the IDF continues targeting Hamas infrastructure despite the ceasefire, indicating ongoing violations and tunnel rehabilitation efforts by Hamas. In Lebanon, the IDF maintains a long-term posture with no withdrawal planned until Hezbollah is disarmed, while the Lebanese army has refused to enter the Ali Taher Ridge area where Hezbollah operatives remain underground. The killing of two IRGC members in Paveh by a newly established group signals potential internal security threats in western Iran. The rejection of the Lebanon-Israel framework agreement by Hezbollah-aligned Lebanese politicians suggests continued resistance to disarmament efforts.
sections.regional_response: Iran has taken a firm stance against negotiations with the US, with the Foreign Ministry explicitly stating no talks will occur at any level in the coming days. The Supreme National Security Council's approval of a US-Iran understanding with 11 positive votes indicates internal political dynamics around potential diplomatic engagement. In Lebanon, the framework agreement with Israel faces strong opposition from Hezbollah-aligned politicians who view it as a shame and surrender, while Iran insists any US agreement must include Lebanese ceasefire and Israeli withdrawal. The Lebanese authority's rejection of Iranian support in favor of the American-Israeli agreement suggests a divergence between Lebanese state actors and Iranian interests.
sections.intelligence_notes: The newly established group Khurri Hiva claiming responsibility for the Paveh attack represents a potential new actor in western Iran's security landscape. The IDF's Arabic-language psychological operations, including the video declaring Hamas is disappearing, indicate ongoing information warfare efforts. Defense Minister Katz's revelation that the IDF had planned a massive aerial campaign against Hezbollah that was stopped by American pressure suggests significant US influence over Israeli military operations. The Lebanese army's refusal to enter the Ali Taher Ridge area highlights the limitations of Lebanese state security forces in confronting Hezbollah positions. The demolition of Shiite village houses in Homs countryside by the al-Jolani government using tanks and bulldozers indicates continued sectarian tensions in Syria.
